claim: Longitudinal MC records are safer and more analytically useful when consent scope and allowed routes are explicit before storage.

missingness.interpretation: Synthetic fixture omits identifying context by design.

revision_reason: Adds a gate that prevents longitudinal data admission when consent scope is ambiguous.
falsification_route: If packets with unknown consent, blocked privacy, or absence-as-evidence pass, the gate is falsified.
next_executable_action: Run validator and wire passing packets only to synthetic test or research-map routes.

claim: A private longitudinal record can enter project memory even when consent basis and retention are unknown.

missingness.interpretation: Consent basis is not available in this packet.

revision_reason: Negative fixture proves the gate rejects unknown consent for longitudinal retention.
falsification_route: Gate is falsified if this packet is accepted into private longitudinal storage.
next_executable_action: Reject and route to consent-review backlog rather than memory.

claim: A missing observation can be interpreted as evidence that no event occurred in the longitudinal record.

missingness.interpretation: This fixture intentionally tries to collapse missingness into negative evidence.

revision_reason: Negative fixture proves missing data cannot be treated as absence.
falsification_route: Gate is falsified if absence_as_evidence true passes validation.
next_executable_action: Reject and preserve as contradiction-ledger example.
